name: Crown Roast of Lamb
recipeIngredient: (2 or more ribs per person, depending on size)
1 Bunch Watercress or curly parsley (for garnish)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 450°F.
2. Make small incisions in the meaty part of the lamb every few inches and insert garlic slivers if using them.
3. Coat the lamb well with olive oil or rub with softened butter, then sprinkle all over with rosemary and pepper.
4. Stuff a ball of aluminum foil into the center of the roast.
5. Place the roast on a small rack in a low roasting pan large enough to hold it, surrounded by potatoes.
6. Lower the oven temperature to 400°F and roast until done, about 30 minutes or until internal temperature reaches 140°F.
7. Remove the foil ball and salt the meat.
8. Transfer to a warmed platter and let rest 5-to-10 minutes before serving.
9. If waiting longer, cover with foil to retain heat.
10. To serve, place paper frills on the rib bones and fill the center with vegetables or stuffing. Surround with potatoes and garnish with watercress or curly parsley. Any sauce would be served on the side. Makes 6 to 8 Servings
